title: Public Company to Private Limited Conversion Online in India
metaDescription: What is public to private conversion? Convert public company to private limited online in India with fees, cost, documents, procedure & expert consultant service.

hero.headline: Public to Private Limited Company Conversion in India – Online Process
hero.subtext: Lower compliance, higher flexibility. End-to-end legal transition by experts.

introduction.heading: Introduction and its Compliance
body: The conversion of a Public Limited Company to a Private Limited Company is a strategic business decision, which is generally taken to have more operational efficiency, lesser compliance requirements and restricted public shareholding. The conversion is subject to approval by the shareholders, Regional Director (RD), alteration in the Articles of Association (AOA) as stipulated under Section 14 of the Companies Act, 2013. The firm is also required to update its statutory books and to notify all supervisory authorities once it is converted. Professionals Law facilitates end to end support in the complete Conversion Process with adherence to laws, correct documentation and timely approvals. Professionals Law gives you the peace of mind of a smooth transition to suit your business objectives.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: A Public Limited Companies often convert into Private Limited Companies to reduce regulatory obligations, avoid public scrutiny and streamline decision-making. Unlike public company, private company do not have to abide the strict SEBI and listing norms, so management of private firm can become more flexible, efficient and cost effective. It is also appropriate for firms seeking to restrict ownership and control to a smaller group. Professionals Law assists companies through this legal transition process in order to ensure that the company complies with the Companies Act and strategic legal advice at each step.

body.title: Reduced Compliance Burden
body.description: Private Limited Companies are exempt from rigorous SEBI and stock exchange regulations, reducing legal and financial overhead.

body.title: Flexibility in Decision-Making
body.description: Smaller shareholder base and less public involvement offer faster, more flexible decision-making.

body.title: Cost-Effective Operations
body.description: Savings on audit, reporting and regulatory compliance make business operations more affordable post-conversion.

body.title: Confidentiality of Financials
body.description: Private companies are not obligated to disclose financial results publicly, preserving competitive advantage.

body.title: Simplified Fundraising
body.description: Allows raising capital through private placements without offering shares to the public.

criteria: A minimum of 2 directors and 2 shareholders
No listed securities on any stock exchange
Approval of shareholders via special resolution is mandatory
Consent from the Regional Director (RD) must be obtained
Revised AOA must reflect restrictions on share transfer and limits on the number of members (max 200)

documents: KYC documents of all the directors and shareholders
Revised Memorandum & Articles of Association (MOA & AOA)
Declaration by Directors and Key Managerial Personnel
List of Creditors with no-objection confirmation
Certificate of Incorporation and PAN of the company

body.description: Pass a resolution proposing the conversion.
Approve the conversion and amendment of AOA through special resolution.
Submit the special resolution with ROC within 30 days.
Draft petition with required annexures and file Form RD-1.
Publish advertisement in English and local newspapers inviting objections (within 21 days).
RD reviews the petition and objections before granting approval.
File the RD order with ROC in Form INC-28.
Receive revised Certificate of Incorporation as a Private Limited Company.
Non-filing or delay in filing MGT-14 or RD-1 may attract a penalty of ₹1,00,000, plus ₹500 per day of default.
Misrepresentation or fraudulent declaration can result in criminal penalties or disqualification of directors.
Failure to obtain RD approval will invalidate the conversion.

body.title: Preparation & EGM
body.description: 5 to 7 Working Days

body.title: RD Application and Newspaper Ads
body.description: 10 to 15 Working Days

body.title: Approval by Regional Director
body.description: 30 to 45 Working Days

body.title: ROC Filings and Final COI
body.description: 5 to 7 Working Days

body.title: Total Time Required
body.description: 45 to 60 Working Days

body.question: Can a listed company convert into a private company?
body.answer: No, listed companies must first delist from the stock exchange before conversion.

body.question: Is NCLT approval required for this conversion?
body.answer: No, approval is granted by the Regional Director (RD), not NCLT.

body.question: Do we need consent from creditors?
body.answer: Yes, a list of creditors with no objection is a mandatory part of the application.

body.question: Will the name of the company change?
body.answer: Yes, “Limited” will be replaced by “Private Limited” in the name.

body.question: What happens if there is an objection to the public notice?
body.answer: The RD will review and adjudicate the objection before approving or rejecting the application.

body.question: Is a new PAN or TAN required post-conversion?
body.answer: No, PAN/TAN remains the same, though the name needs to be updated.

body.question: Can the process be handled online?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law can complete the process remotely on your behalf.

body.question: How long does the conversion take?
body.answer: On average, the process takes 45 to 60 working days.

body.question: Does Professionals Law provide post-conversion support?
body.answer: Absolutely, Professionals Law assists with updating statutory registers, bank records and compliance filings.
